ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for packaged honey lemon RTD beverage; closest match is honey lemon beverage class. Packaged/RTD qualifier not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKN covers honey-based beverages broadly; no specific code for honey-lemon RTD packaged drink. Used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for packaged honey lemon beverage as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one bottle), providing about 87.5 kcal and 21.9 g of carbohydrates, predominantly from added honey and sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Honey Lemon (Packaged) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Honey Lemon (Packaged), record ID D05010024. All per-100g values are as reported. Saturated fat not separately reported by HPB for this item; set to 0.0 as fat is 0.0g/100g by formulation.

gi_source.title: Honey, GI value 58 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged honey-lemon RTD beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 58 (medium) is estimated by analogy to honey (GI 58, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which is the dominant glycaemic driver in this beverage. Lemon juice contributes negligible additional glycaemic load. Glycemic load per 250 ml serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (58 × 21.9) / 100 = 12.7, rounded to 12.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (8.16 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage; sodium is low at 12 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low (12 mg/100 g), the sugar content of 8.16 g/100 g classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB); regular SSB consumption is independently associated with elevated blood pressure (AHA 2018), and individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this drink in favour of unsweetened alternatives such as plain water or fresh lemon water without added sweeteners.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.16 g sugar/100 g, medium GI (58), zero dietary fibre, and rapid liquid glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: This packaged honey-lemon drink is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.16 g of free sugars per 100 g, a medium GI of 58, and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugars are absorbed rapidly and cause acute postprandial blood glucose elevation, making this drink unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, who should instead choose unsweetened beverages.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption linked to increased CVD risk; sugar 8.16 g/100 g with zero fat and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While this beverage contains no fat, no saturated fat, and very low sodium (12 mg/100 g), its classification as a sugar-sweetened beverage (8.16 g sugar/100 g) is associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk through mechanisms including dyslipidaemia, insulin resistance, and systemic inflammation; individuals with CVD or at elevated CVD risk should limit SSB intake and choose unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Lemon Drink (Packaged) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink (RTD) beverage widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is formulated from water, honey, and lemon juice, often with added sugar or glucose syrup to standardise sweetness and extend shelf life. The drink has a light golden-yellow colour, a sweet-tart flavour profile dominated by floral honey notes balanced by the mild acidity of lemon, and a thin, watery texture typical of RTD juice-style beverages. Per 100 ml, it provides 35 kcal, 8.76 g of carbohydrates (of which 8.16 g are sugars), negligible protein (0 g), and negligible fat (0 g), with a low sodium content of 12 mg. The glycemic index is estimated at 58 (medium) by analogy to honey, and a standard 250 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 12.8. Clinically, this drink is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is not recommended for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high free-sugar content and rapid glycaemic impact. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable but is not vegan due to honey content. In Singapore, it is commonly consumed as a refreshment or mild throat-soothing drink and is sold in bottles and cans in supermarkets, convenience stores, and hawker centres.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a bottle of honey lemon drink?
Is packaged honey lemon drink suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of honey lemon beverage?
Is honey lemon drink good for sore throat?
Is honey lemon RTD drink halal?
What is the GI of honey lemon drink?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D05010024), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros are confirmed. GI is estimated by analogy to honey from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), which is an approved GI source; the estimation basis is clearly documented.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D05010024; this is a sugar-water-based beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D05010024; this beverage contains no fat-bearing ingredients by formulation. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 as a direct consequence of fat_g being 0.0 (confirmed zero). dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D05010024; the beverage contains no fibre-bearing ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB Foodo for this beverage item. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported by HPB Foodo for this packaged beverage entry as of 2026. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on honey (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283); no direct GI measurement for this specific RTD product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 250 ml (250 g) serving: (58 × 21.9) / 100 = 12.7, reported as 12.8 after rounding. The input record listed GI confidence of 0.341 from an 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved source; GI has been re-anchored to the University of Sydney GI Database via analogous honey estimate. is_vegan set to false because honey is an animal-derived product.
